session.feature_description: An AI assistant that drafts client-facing legal emails from short bullet-point notes provided by lawyers.

release_spec.problem: Lawyers spend significant time drafting routine but sensitive client emails, which must be precise, compliant, and tone-appropriate.

personas.name: Amelia
needs: Draft high-quality emails faster without losing nuance
Ensure language is client-friendly but legally sound
pain_points: Even simple updates take long to draft and review
High cognitive load switching between cases

personas.name: Jonas
needs: Consistency of tone and legal accuracy across the team
Confidence that AI-generated text won’t breach confidentiality
pain_points: Fear of accidental disclosure or misstatements

core_use_cases: Generate a first-draft email summarising case status for a client
Turn bullet-point notes into a follow-up email after a meeting
Produce variations of an email for internal vs external audiences
user_stories: As an associate, I want to paste bullet notes and get a high-quality draft email so I can edit instead of writing from scratch.
As a GC, I want configurable tone and disclaimer templates so I can ensure communications follow policy.
As a paralegal, I want the system to flag when my draft touches sensitive case facts so I can seek approval before sending.
non_functional_requirements: Generated emails must never include data from other clients.
System must respond within a few seconds for a typical prompt.
All outputs must be editable before sending – no auto-sending.
success_metrics: Reduce time spent drafting routine emails by ~40–60%.
Decrease number of communication style revisions requested by senior staff.
Zero confirmed incidents of cross-client data leakage.

risks.title: Cross-matter or cross-client data leakage
risks.description: Model may accidentally include details from other matters or clients when drafting, especially when prompted with ambiguous context.
mitigation_ideas: Scope training and prompts to per-tenant or per-matter context only.
Add post-processing filters for names and IDs not in the current matter.
Audit random samples of drafts for leakage.

risks.title: Incorrect legal statements or promises
risks.description: AI may generate language that inadvertently creates legal obligations, makes incorrect representations of law, or contradicts actual case facts.
mitigation_ideas: Enforce mandatory human review before any external sending.
Add cautious hedging language to all AI-generated drafts.
Restrict the model from stating legal conclusions without human qualification.

risks.title: Tone or formality mismatch
risks.description: Drafted emails may be too casual or overly formal, misrepresenting the firm’s professional voice or the relationship with a specific client.
mitigation_ideas: Allow users to set tone preferences (formal, neutral, empathetic).
Include per-client communication history as context.

risks.title: Over-reliance reducing professional judgment
risks.description: Lawyers may accept AI drafts without sufficient review, eroding critical professional skills and increasing error rates over time.
mitigation_ideas: Persistent AI-generated label on all drafts.
Highlight fields that contain AI-inferred information.
Track edit rates and alert when users accept drafts without modification.

checklist.item: Per-matter context scoping confirmed with engineering
Tone templates reviewed and approved by senior legal staff
Mandatory review workflow implemented — no auto-send path exists
Cross-client leakage test suite executed with pass/fail criteria
Legal accuracy review completed by in-house counsel on sample outputs
User guide explaining AI limitations and review obligations distributed to all users
AI-generated label persistent and clearly visible in all draft views
Random audit sampling mechanism in place for post-deployment monitoring

levels.description: Test individual prompt templates, tone selector, and disclaimer injection independently.
notes: Mock the LLM for deterministic tests on prompt assembly.
Verify disclaimer text is appended for all output variants.

levels.description: Verify integration with identity system, matter management, and email clients.
notes: Check that only allowed matter context is accessible per user.
Verify mandatory review step cannot be bypassed via API.

levels.description: Simulate lawyer drafting process end-to-end: bullet notes → AI draft → human edit → send.
notes: Include supervisor review flows in scenarios.
Test across different user roles (associate, GC, paralegal).

levels.description: Assess AI-generated content for cross-client leakage, legal inaccuracy, and tone misalignment.
notes: Use a scenario library with sensitive and edge-case prompts.
Score drafts on accuracy, tone, and absence of forbidden content.

test_cases.name: Cross-client leakage detection
test_cases.abstract_input: Draft emails for two different fictitious clients in the same session and check that names, case IDs, and facts never cross.
test_cases.expected_behavior: No references to entities outside the active matter appear in any draft.

test_cases.name: Legal accuracy under ambiguous input
test_cases.abstract_input: Provide bullet points containing ambiguous legal status (e.g., ‘case is almost settled’) and review the AI’s generated language.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Assistant uses cautious hedging language and avoids definitive legal conclusions when context is incomplete.

test_cases.name: Mandatory review enforcement
test_cases.abstract_input: Attempt to send an AI-generated email without any edit or review action.
test_cases.expected_behavior: System prevents sending or requires an explicit supervisor approval step before email is dispatched.

test_cases.name: Tone mismatch detection
test_cases.abstract_input: Submit bullet notes for a formal litigation matter with tone selector set to default.
test_cases.expected_behavior: Draft maintains professional formality; overly casual phrasing flagged in the UI.

test_cases.name: AI-generated label visibility
test_cases.abstract_input: Open any AI-generated draft in the editing interface.
test_cases.expected_behavior: A persistent, prominent label reading ‘AI-generated – review required’ is visible throughout the editing flow and cannot be dismissed.

guardrails.name: Matter-scoped context enforcement
guardrails.description: Restrict all retrieval and prompt context to the currently active matter ID only.
guardrails.implementation_idea: Use matter ID scoping on all vector/db queries and enforce tenant boundary checks before any context is injected into the LLM prompt.

guardrails.name: Mandatory review gate
guardrails.description: Block all outbound email paths until a qualified reviewer marks the draft as reviewed.
guardrails.implementation_idea: Disable the Send button until the reviewer checks a confirmation checkbox. Log reviewer identity and timestamp to the audit trail.

guardrails.name: Persistent AI-generated banner
guardrails.description: Display a non-dismissible banner on all AI-generated drafts indicating they require human review.
guardrails.implementation_idea: Inject a fixed, styled banner at the top of every draft view. The banner cannot be hidden by CSS overrides from user settings.

guardrails.name: Tone and banned-phrase post-filter
guardrails.description: Scan generated drafts for disallowed phrases and flag tone misalignments before presenting to the user.
guardrails.implementation_idea: Rule-based filter for known banned phrases (e.g., definitive settlement amounts without qualification). Future: lightweight classifier for formality scoring.

release_notes.title: AI Legal Email Drafting Assistant – v0.1.0
release_notes.summary: Draft routine client emails faster while keeping humans firmly in control. This release introduces a governed AI drafting workflow purpose-built for legal environments.
whats_new: Generate first-draft legal emails from bullet notes in seconds.
Matter-scoped context enforcement to reduce cross-client leakage risk.
Mandatory review workflows and persistent AI-generated labels baked into the UX.
Tone selector with formal, neutral, and empathetic presets.
why_it_matters: Frees up lawyers’ time for higher-value work by eliminating routine drafting overhead.
Reduces inconsistency in client communications across the team.
Introduces repeatable structure and review obligations around AI usage in legal workflows.
known_limitations: Model does not understand jurisdiction-specific nuances perfectly – human review is mandatory.
Cross-client leakage guardrails require proper matter ID tagging in the source system.

landing_copy.hero_title: Draft legal emails in minutes, not hours.
landing_copy.hero_subtitle: Turn bullet-point notes into client-ready drafts – with governance built in.
landing_copy.tagline: Speed up communication without losing control.
key_benefits: Save hours on routine legal emails.
Keep humans in charge of final wording.
Reduce risk with matter-scoped context and review gates.
Consistent tone across the team, configurable per matter type.

feature_sections.title: From notes to drafts
feature_sections.body: Paste bullet points, get structured, client-friendly drafts ready for review. The assistant adapts tone to your firm’s communication style.

feature_sections.title: Governance-aware by design
feature_sections.body: Review gates, disclaimers, and risk-aligned tests are built into the assistant — not bolted on afterwards.

trust_and_safety.headline: Designed for sensitive legal communications
bullets: Matter-scoped context to reduce cross-client leakage.
Mandatory human review for all external emails.
Clear disclaimers that preserve professional judgment.

landing_copy.cta: Run a readiness check for this feature

slides.title: The Problem – Email drafting overhead in legal
slides.objective: Establish the pain point clearly so the audience recognises the problem before the solution is introduced.
key_points: Lawyers spend 20–40% of their time on routine but high-stakes written communication.
Each email carries legal and confidentiality risk that generic AI tools don’t account for.
Current tools offer speed but no governance — unacceptable in regulated environments.
notes_for_speaker: Open with a relatable example: an associate spending 45 minutes on a two-paragraph case update.
Emphasise the tension: speed matters, but so do accuracy and confidentiality.
Set up the next slide by asking: what if you could have both?

slides.title: The Solution – Governed AI Drafting
slides.objective: Introduce the product as a governed workflow, not just a faster way to write emails.
key_points: Bullet notes in → polished draft out — in seconds, not minutes.
Matter-scoped context prevents cross-client leakage at the infrastructure level.
Mandatory review gates mean lawyers stay accountable for every word sent.
notes_for_speaker: Lead with the workflow demo if live, or a screenshot sequence.
Stress that this is a drafting assistant, not an autonomous sender — lawyer is always in the loop.
Mention the tone selector as a signal this is built for legal, not adapted from a generic tool.

slides.title: The Risk Architecture – How ReleaseOps stress-tested it
slides.objective: Show that the system was built with a structured risk framework, not shipped with fingers crossed.
key_points: Navigator identified 4 risks across Privacy, Safety, and UX/Business dimensions.
Sentinel produced 5 test cases directly linked to R1–R4 — every risk has a test.
4 guardrails implemented: matter scoping, review gate, AI label, phrase filter.
notes_for_speaker: This slide is your credibility builder with risk/compliance audiences.
Show the traceability chain: R1 → T1+T3 → G1+G2 — nothing is untested.
If time allows, open the Tests tab in ReleaseOps to show the matrix live.

slides.title: Impact & Next Steps
slides.objective: Close with concrete outcomes and a clear call to action.
key_points: Target: 40–60% reduction in routine drafting time.
Zero cross-client incidents — measurable safety target built into the spec.
Next: pilot with 5 associates, collect edit rates, and refine tone models.
notes_for_speaker: Anchor the pitch to the success metrics from the release spec.
Invite the audience to run their own feature through ReleaseOps to see the pipeline in action.
End with: ‘This is what release-ready looks like for AI in legal.’
